mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.5 percentage points within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger risk-off positioning, depressing S&P 500 prices over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of industrial production and residential housing starts indicates a synchronized demand shock that overwhelms supply-side resilience, leading to earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve model R-squared by at least 0.02 over a baseline single-proxy model, or forward returns do not differ significantly between high/low interaction quartiles.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accelerating real GDP growth, rising real consumer spending acts as a leading indicator of corporate revenue expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns in high-GDP-growth regimes are statistically indistinguishable from baseline, or the signal exhibits negative predictive power during the specified regime window.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust economic momentum that supports corporate earnings expectations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no significant difference between payroll growth deciles.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, the cost of capital becomes restrictive enough to compress equity multiples, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where the rate exceeds the threshold, or the signal's predictive power vanishes after controlling for inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising industrial production and stable unemployment indicates a supply-led growth phase where capacity utilization expands without triggering wage inflation, supporting equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-proxy baselines in out-of-sample testing, or forward returns are not significantly higher when both conditions are met simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where CPI exceeds 3.0%, rising real consumer spending signals persistent demand-pull pressures that force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, ultimately depressing short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant negative skew when inflation exceeds the threshold, or the signal reverses direction during disinflationary periods.
